coaching.coaching: Plan how verification criterion for loop diagram deliverable will be verified in-session — restate any deferred or unobservable success target as an observable proxy.
coaching.subject: verification criterion for loop diagram deliverable

coaching.coaching: Pin down concrete grep command for AC1 verification with a concrete worked example.
coaching.subject: concrete grep command for AC1 verification

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC that verifies the loop diagram is present in the new section of `docs/passing-code-review.md` (e.g., 'the file contains a fenced code block illustrating the `rebar review-code` → iterate loop; verified by grep/reading').
evidence: Scope bullet: 'docs/passing-code-review.md  (new "Preview the review locally" section + intro cross-link + loop diagram)'

AC1 verifies only 'a section teaching rebar review-code...incl. the [agents] extra + REBAR_VERIFY_ENABLE_CODE_REVIEW enabling requirement; verified by reading the rendered file' — the loop diagram is not named.
AC4 checks anchor resolution, not diagram presence.
ABSENCE: No AC says 'the loop diagram is present' or 'verified by reading the diagram block'.
findings.finding: The Scope names 'loop diagram' as a sub-deliverable for docs/passing-code-review.md, but no AC verifies its presence — it is an in-scope content hunk without a verifying criterion.
findings.location: Scope: 'docs/passing-code-review.md (new "Preview the review locally" section + intro cross-link + loop diagram)'
scenarios: An implementer adds the section text and the anchor but omits the loop diagram; all four ACs pass green and the diagram silently goes missing.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Acceptance Criteria: '- [ ] `docs/passing-code-review.md` new section contains a fenced-code loop diagram showing the `rebar review-code` → amend → re-run cycle (mirroring the `writing-a-passing-plan.md` diagram); verified by reading the rendered file.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an acceptance criterion (or extend AC1) that explicitly verifies the loop diagram is present in docs/passing-code-review.md, e.g. by grepping for the diagram marker or confirming its rendering.
evidence: Scope names three sub-deliverables for docs/passing-code-review.md: the new section, the intro cross-link, and the loop diagram.
AC1 verifies section content (rebar review-code instruction, [agents] extra, REBAR_VERIFY_ENABLE_CODE_REVIEW); AC4 verifies the anchor link for the intro cross-link.
No AC mentions, tests, or verifies the loop diagram.
findings.finding: The 'loop diagram' sub-deliverable listed in Scope for docs/passing-code-review.md has no covering acceptance criterion, leaving it unverifiable and possibly unimplemented without detection.
findings.location: Scope — 'docs/passing-code-review.md (new "Preview the review locally" section + intro cross-link + loop diagram)'
scenarios: An implementer omits the loop diagram; no AC fails; the gap is undetected at review time.
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC1: '…; the section includes the loop diagram illustrating the push→review→fix→push cycle; verified by reading the rendered file.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace AC1's 'verified by reading the rendered file' with a concrete grep command that checks for each required element (e.g., `grep -E 'rebar review-code' docs/passing-code-review.md && grep -E 'REBAR_VERIFY_ENABLE_CODE_REVIEW' docs/passing-code-review.md && grep -E '\[agents\]' docs/passing-code-review.md`).
evidence: No grep or structured command given for AC1 unlike ACs 2 and 3
findings.finding: AC1's proving method is 'verified by reading the rendered file', which is a subjective manual check rather than a concrete, reproducible command. A grep for the key required terms would make this machine-verifiable and remove reliance on human judgment about completeness.
scenarios: An executor marks AC1 done after skimming the file without verifying all three required elements (rebar review-code, [agents] extra, REBAR_VERIFY_ENABLE_CODE_REVIEW) are present — the vague 'reading' check does not catch omissions.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC1: 'verified by `grep -E 'rebar review-code' docs/passing-code-review.md && grep 'REBAR_VERIFY_ENABLE_CODE_REVIEW' docs/passing-code-review.md && grep '\[agents\]' docs/passing-code-review.md`'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the exact intended heading text for the new section in the plan (e.g., 'Preview the review locally') so AC4's anchor-match verification has a fixed target; alternatively, replace 'verified by matching the GitHub-generated anchor to the heading text' with a concrete check such as `grep -F '(#preview-the-review-locally)' docs/passing-code-review.md` using the expected GitHub-slugified anchor.
evidence: 'verified by matching the GitHub-generated anchor to the heading text'
The plan names only 'new section heading' without specifying the heading string
The intro anchor link target is unspecified
findings.finding: AC4 says the anchor link is 'verified by matching the GitHub-generated anchor to the heading text', but the plan never states the exact heading text for the new section. Without the heading text, the executor must choose it arbitrarily and then derive the anchor — there is no fixed target to match against, making the verification a tautology (any heading matches its own anchor).
scenarios: Executor picks any heading text, writes the anchor to match, and AC4 passes vacuously — the real risk (a broken anchor in the intro cross-link) is never caught.
findings.suggested_fix: Specify the heading text in Scope or AC4, e.g., 'new section heading: ## Preview the review locally', and update AC4 to: 'verified by `grep -F "(#preview-the-review-locally)" docs/passing-code-review.md`'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or expand AC4) to verify that the *introduction* of `docs/passing-code-review.md` contains a prose cross-link to the new 'Preview the review locally' section — not just that the anchor is syntactically valid.
evidence: Scope bullet: 'intro cross-link' listed as a distinct deliverable alongside the new section and loop diagram.

AC4 says 'The intro anchor link resolves to the new section heading; verified by matching the GitHub-generated anchor to the heading text' — this checks only that the anchor target is valid, not that a cross-link prose sentence is added at the top of the file.
ABSENCE: No AC says 'the introduction of passing-code-review.md mentions / links to the new section'.
findings.finding: The Scope names 'intro cross-link' as a sub-deliverable for docs/passing-code-review.md, but no AC verifies that a cross-link to the new section is added in the file's introduction.
findings.location: Scope: 'docs/passing-code-review.md (new "Preview the review locally" section + intro cross-link + loop diagram)'
scenarios: An implementer adds a working anchor on the heading but does not add any introductory prose pointing readers to the new section; AC4 passes (anchor resolves) but the cross-link discoverability goal is unmet.
findings.suggested_fix: Revise AC4 to: 'The intro paragraph of `docs/passing-code-review.md` contains a link whose anchor resolves to the new "Preview the review locally" section heading; verified by (a) grep for the anchor text and (b) confirming it appears before the first `##` section heading.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
